main.h(3) Library Functions Manual main.h(3)

NAME

main.h - Global application exit and signal handling API.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


bool should_exit (void)
void signal_exit (void)
void set_interrupt_callback (void(*cb)(void))
void setup_signal_handlers (void)

Variables


atomic_t g_should_exit

Detailed Description

Global application exit and signal handling API.

Provides a centralized exit mechanism and signal handler registration used by all modes (client, mirror, discovery, server) to coordinate graceful shutdown. All modes share a single global exit flag and interrupt callback.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

Definition in file main.h.

Function Documentation

void set_interrupt_callback (void(* cb )(void))

Register a mode-specific interrupt callback

Called by mode-specific code to register a callback that will be invoked when signal_exit() is called. Used to shut down network sockets so threads blocked in recv() unblock quickly instead of waiting for timeouts.

The callback is called synchronously from signal_exit(), so it must be async-signal-safe (only atomics, socket_shutdown(), no malloc/etc).

Only one callback can be registered at a time. Setting a new callback replaces the previous one.

Parameters

cb Function to call on exit signal, or NULL to unregister

Definition at line 218 of file main.c.

218                                               {
219   g_interrupt_callback = cb;
220 }




Referenced by client_main(), and
  discovery_main().






void
  setup_signal_handlers (void )


Set up global signal handlers




Called once at program startup in main() BEFORE mode
    dispatch. Registers:


    
  
  • SIGTERM → graceful shutdown with logging
    • 
  
  • SIGPIPE → ignore (prevent crashes from broken pipes)
    • 
  
  • SIGUSR1 → synchronization debugging output (debug builds only)
    • 
  
  • SIGUSR2 → memory report (debug builds only)
    • 
  
  • Console Ctrl+C handler (all platforms)
    • 



SIGWINCH (terminal resize) is NOT registered here - client mode
    registers its own handler because it needs special processing.




Set up global signal handlers Called once at startup before mode
    dispatch


Definition at line 337 of file main.c.


337                                  {
338   platform_set_console_ctrl_handler(console_ctrl_handler);
339 
340 #ifndef _WIN32
341   platform_signal_handler_t handlers[] = {
342       {SIGINT, handle_sigterm},
343       {SIGTERM, handle_sigterm},
344       {SIGPIPE, SIG_IGN},
345   };
346   platform_register_signal_handlers(handlers, 3);
347   log_debug("Signal handlers registered");
348 #endif
349 }






References log_debug,
    platform_register_signal_handlers(), and
    platform_set_console_ctrl_handler().


Referenced by main().






bool should_exit (void )


Check if the application should exit




Called by mode main loops to detect shutdown requests from signals
    or errors.




Returns


true if shutdown has been requested, false
  otherwise


Definition at line 202 of file main.c.


202                        {
203   return atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit);
204 }






References atomic_load_bool(), and
  g_should_exit.


Referenced by client_main(),
    connection_attempt_tcp(), connection_attempt_websocket(),
    display_full_reset(), main(),
    server_connection_establish(), session_client_like_run(),
    session_pipeline_run_main(), and session_render_loop().






void
  signal_exit (void )


Signal that the application should exit




Sets the global exit flag and calls the registered interrupt
    callback if set. Safe to call from signal handlers (uses only atomics and
    function pointers). Called by signal handlers (SIGTERM, Ctrl+C) and normal
    code (errors, timeouts).


Definition at line 206 of file main.c.


206                        {
207   // Note: This function may be called from a signal handler context where
208   // other threads may hold mutex locks. We avoid log_console() here to prevent
209   // deadlock (render loop holds terminal lock, signal handler can't acquire it).
210   // The shutdown will be logged by normal thread context later.
211   atomic_store_bool(&g_should_exit, true);
212   void (*cb)(void) = g_interrupt_callback;
213   if (cb) {
214     cb();
215   }
216 }






References atomic_store_bool(), and
  g_should_exit.


Referenced by client_main(), main(), and
    session_render_loop().








Variable
  Documentation




atomic_t g_should_exit [extern]


Global exit flag used by all modes




Set to true when the application should exit (SIGTERM, Ctrl+C,
    error, etc). All modes check this flag regularly to exit gracefully.




Global shutdown flag referenced by lib/logging.c and
    lib/debug/lock.c In production builds, this is defined in main.c and set
    during shutdown. For tests, we stub it out as false (tests don't perform
    shutdown).
